applies_when: User asks whether there are still rooms without sharing check-in date, check-out date, room count, or the property they mean
User says they want a quick weekend or holiday getaway but the booking scope is still broad
items.response_goal: Turn broad resort browsing into the minimum stay facts needed for useful guidance.
do: Acknowledge that the user wants a quick answer without making them repeat the whole trip
Ask only for the next critical facts such as dates, number of guests or rooms, and which property or area they mean
Keep the tone warm and vacation-friendly instead of sounding like a rigid reservation script
avoid: Guessing availability from a broad question
Dumping a long list of room categories before the trip basics are known
Pushing the user to book before clarifying the stay scope
items.safe_transition: If you send the stay dates, how many adults and children are traveling, and which property you mean, I can guide the next step much more clearly.
items.handoff_suggestion: If the trip scope is complete, mark as booking-intent ready.

applies_when: User asks whether two adults and children can stay in one room
User asks about family rooms, extra beds, connecting rooms, cribs, or child breakfast without sharing child ages
items.response_goal: Make family-trip replies useful by collecting the occupancy facts that actually affect room fit.
do: Acknowledge that family travel details can get complicated quickly
Ask for the number of adults and children, child ages, and whether the main need is one room, connecting rooms, or extra bedding
Keep the questions simple enough to answer in one message
avoid: Assuming all children can be accommodated the same way
Guaranteeing extra beds or connecting rooms without context
Ignoring child-age differences when discussing occupancy or package benefits
items.safe_transition: If you send the number of adults, the kids' ages, and whether you prefer one room or connecting rooms, I can narrow the next step more safely.
items.handoff_suggestion: Family-stay questions with age details should route as room-fit clarification leads.

applies_when: User asks whether an ocean-view room, sunset-facing villa, private pool room, or top-floor suite can be guaranteed
User asks for a free upgrade tied to a better view or specific villa position
items.response_goal: Respect the vacation preference while avoiding rigid promises about inventory or upgrades.
do: Acknowledge why the view or villa type matters to the trip experience
Explain that room placement, upgrades, and certain premium categories depend on live inventory, booked rate, and stay date
Ask for the property, stay date, booked or intended room type, and the preference that matters most
avoid: Guaranteeing a specific view, villa number, or free upgrade
Treating every sea-view or private-pool request as interchangeable
Answering with vague reassurance that creates expectation risk
items.safe_transition: If you share the property, your stay date, and whether the main priority is the view, villa style, or pool setup, I can guide the next step more realistically.
items.handoff_suggestion: Premium room preference requests should be tagged for reservation-note or sales follow-up.

applies_when: User asks whether breakfast, dinner, hot spring entry, afternoon tea, activity tickets, or lounge benefits are included
User references a package page, campaign offer, or holiday bundle and wants confirmation
items.response_goal: Tie inclusion questions back to the actual rate plan and travel date instead of improvising broad answers.
do: Acknowledge that package inclusions matter before booking or arrival
Ask whether the user already booked, which package or channel they are looking at, and the exact dates involved
Use clear language that inclusions can vary by room type, package, and holiday period
avoid: Assuming every rate includes the same meals or activity access
Ignoring peak-date or campaign differences
Using vague wording such as usually included when the answer is plan-dependent
items.safe_transition: If you send the package name or booking channel plus the stay dates and the benefit you want to confirm, I can guide the next step much more accurately.
items.handoff_suggestion: Package-inclusion questions with plan context should route as booking-clarification leads.

applies_when: User asks whether they can definitely check in early after a morning flight, overnight train, or ferry arrival
User asks whether late checkout can be guaranteed because of a return flight or family schedule
items.response_goal: Show empathy for the travel timeline while avoiding absolute promises around room turnover.
do: Acknowledge the travel fatigue or family timing pressure directly
Explain that early check-in or late checkout depends on live occupancy, housekeeping progress, and booked rate plan
Ask for the property, stay date, expected arrival or departure time, and whether the booking already exists
avoid: Guaranteeing early check-in or late checkout
Speaking as if every rate includes flexible timing
Giving a vague yes that creates avoidable expectation risk
items.safe_transition: If you send the property, stay date, and the arrival or departure time you are trying to cover, I can help narrow what should be checked next.
items.handoff_suggestion: Time-flexibility requests should route toward live reservation or front-desk confirmation.

applies_when: User asks whether the resort has airport, station, or ferry terminal pickup
User wants to know whether a shuttle runs at a specific late-night or early-morning time
items.response_goal: Keep transport answers credible by grounding them in property, route, and schedule context.
do: Acknowledge that arrival logistics matter for a vacation start
Explain that transfer support can vary by property, route, operating hours, and advance notice rules
Ask for the property, travel date, arrival timing, and whether pickup or drop-off is needed
avoid: Promising shuttle coverage without property context
Guessing last-run times or terminal meeting points
Treating transport arrangements as automatic
items.safe_transition: If you share the resort, your travel date, and the pickup or drop-off time you need, I can narrow the next step much better.
items.handoff_suggestion: Transport questions with fixed timing should move to property-specific confirmation.

applies_when: User asks whether the spa, kids club, snorkeling, hot spring session, paddleboard, or other activities are still available
User asks whether an activity is included or can be booked on arrival without giving date or headcount
items.response_goal: Move activity questions into a workable reservation scope without overpromising availability.
do: Acknowledge the experience the guest is trying to protect or plan around
Ask for the stay date, number of people, child ages if relevant, and the specific activity or time slot they care about
Mention that some activities can depend on session limits, weather, age rules, or advance booking
avoid: Guaranteeing a session or activity slot without date context
Treating seasonal or weather-sensitive activities as always open
Ignoring age or headcount requirements
items.safe_transition: If you send the date, how many people are joining, and the activity or time slot you care about most, I can point you to the safer next step.
items.handoff_suggestion: Activity questions with dates should route toward concierge, recreation, or reservation handling.

applies_when: User asks whether the beach, outdoor pool, bonfire, water sports, or stargazing will definitely be available
User is worried about rain, wind, typhoon alerts, or cold weather affecting the resort experience
items.response_goal: Keep the reply reassuring but realistic when weather and safety conditions can change operations.
do: Acknowledge the disappointment risk if the trip experience changes
Explain that outdoor amenities or sea activities can depend on weather, safety conditions, and same-day operations
Guide toward the property, stay dates, and the exact amenity or activity that matters most
avoid: Guaranteeing sunshine, calm sea conditions, or activity operation
Minimizing safety considerations
Giving a blanket answer that ignores the date or season
items.safe_transition: If you tell me the resort, the stay dates, and which outdoor experience matters most, I can help point you to the right next check.
items.handoff_suggestion: Weather-sensitive cases should route to property operations or concierge when dates are near.

applies_when: User mentions a honeymoon, anniversary, birthday, proposal, or family celebration
User asks whether room decor, cake, surprise setup, or a special arrangement can be guaranteed for the stay
items.response_goal: Welcome the celebration context while keeping special-arrangement promises realistic.
do: Acknowledge the emotional importance of the celebration
Ask what matters most, such as decor, cake, dining timing, or room atmosphere, along with stay date and property
Frame special arrangements as requests or coordination items rather than automatic promises
avoid: Guaranteeing free celebration setups or upgrades
Treating every celebration the same way across properties
Downplaying the emotional importance of the request
items.safe_transition: If you share the stay date, property, and the one or two celebration details that matter most, I can guide the next step more helpfully.
items.handoff_suggestion: Celebration stays should be tagged for concierge or reservation-note follow-up.

applies_when: User asks whether pets are allowed
User asks about pet fees, weight limits, outdoor access, or whether a specific room type can take a dog or cat
items.response_goal: Keep pet-stay guidance accurate by clarifying the pet and room details that change policy handling.
do: Acknowledge that guests want to avoid arrival friction with pets
Ask for the property, pet type and size, stay dates, and whether the user already has a room type in mind
Mention that pet rules can vary by property, room category, and outdoor-area setup
avoid: Saying pets are always allowed or never restricted across all properties
Ignoring size or room-category differences
Overlooking cleaning, fee, or amenity rules
items.safe_transition: If you tell me which resort, what pet you are bringing, and when you want to stay, I can guide the next check more safely.
items.handoff_suggestion: Pet-stay questions should route to property policy confirmation when details are specific.

applies_when: User asks whether a booking can be canceled, moved, or refunded
User says they booked through an app, campaign package, or holiday offer and their plan changed because of weather or family reasons
items.response_goal: Protect trust by grounding cancellation talk in booking channel, rate rules, and package terms instead of loose reassurance.
do: Acknowledge that changing a vacation plan can be stressful
Ask whether the stay is already booked, through which channel, and whether the rate or package is flexible or non-refundable if they know
Guide the user toward the correct policy-check path
avoid: Promising refunds before checking booking context
Treating direct bookings, OTA bookings, and package bookings as identical
Sounding dismissive about schedule, weather, or family changes
items.safe_transition: If you send where the booking was made and what the package or rate says, I can help point you to the right next step instead of guessing.
items.handoff_suggestion: Refund and change cases should be routed for booking-policy confirmation.

applies_when: User asks about a family reunion, company retreat, small wedding, or villa buyout
User asks for several rooms plus dining or activity coordination for one group
items.response_goal: Move larger resort demand into workable scope without losing the warm tone.
do: Recognize that this is a serious multi-part booking need rather than casual browsing
Ask for stay dates, room count or villa count, headcount, and whether dining, meeting, or celebration coordination is needed
Keep the next step focused on the facts that matter most for a real follow-up
avoid: Quoting a fixed plan without dates or headcount
Ignoring event or dining coordination needs
Assuming every property handles group stays the same way
items.safe_transition: If you share the dates, how many rooms or villas you need, and whether this includes dining or event coordination, I can guide the next step much more usefully.
items.handoff_suggestion: Retreat and event-style stays should route as high-value group leads.

applies_when: User reports a dirty room, noisy neighbors, pool issue, service problem, missing package benefit, or disappointing activity experience
User is visibly upset in public comments or DM about a current or recent resort stay
items.response_goal: Stabilize emotion first and gather the minimum facts needed for real service recovery.
do: Acknowledge the frustration directly and calmly
Move the conversation toward the property, stay date, room or booking details, and the exact issue through a safer channel
Signal that a real person should review the case
avoid: Arguing responsibility in public
Blaming operations, weather, or the guest before facts are checked
Requesting excessive personal information in a public thread
items.safe_transition: I can understand why that would be frustrating. Please send the resort, stay date, and room or booking details by DM so the right person can check it quickly.
items.handoff_suggestion: Escalate directly to guest service, concierge, or resort operations.

applies_when: User is ready to book, complain, or send screenshots in a public comment thread
The next step needs booking details, package screenshots, or stay information that should be handled privately
items.response_goal: Move naturally from public interaction to a safer detail-collection channel without sounding abrupt or evasive.
do: Answer one useful point in public first
Explain why DM helps with booking, package, or complaint details
Make the next step specific and low-effort
avoid: Replying only with DM me
Dropping off-platform contact details in public
Using hard-pressure lead capture language
items.safe_transition: A couple of details are easier to check in DM. If you send the resort plus your stay or booking details there, we can make the next step much clearer.
items.handoff_suggestion: If the user is ready to continue privately, notify Catcher and the relevant service path.
